entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: Spanish peanut (Arachis hypogae L.), the crop being studied for seed dormancy characteristics.

entities.name: FRESH SEED DORMANCY
entities.description: The ability of seeds to remain dormant immediately after harvest to prevent premature germination.

entities.name: POD YIELD
entities.description: The quantity of pods produced, which can be reduced by in situ germination.

entities.name: SEED QUALITY
entities.description: The overall quality of the seeds, which can be compromised by in situ germination.

entities.name: IN SITU GERMINATION
entities.description: The process of seeds germinating while still in the pod or on the plant in the field.

entities.name: UNPREDICTABLE RAINFALL ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Environmental conditions with irregular rainfall that trigger in situ germination.

entities.name: INHERITANCE
entities.description: The genetic transmission of traits from parents to offspring, specifically regarding seed dormancy.

entities.name: ICGV 86158
entities.description: A dormant Spanish peanut genotype used in the study.

entities.name: ICGV 87378
entities.description: A dormant Spanish peanut genotype used in the study.

entities.name: JL 24
entities.description: A non-dormant Spanish peanut genotype used in the study.

entities.name: GERMINATION TEST
entities.description: A laboratory evaluation conducted at 35 ± 2°C in the dark to determine seed dormancy.

entities.name: DOMINANT ALLELE
entities.description: A single gene allele that controls the expression of fresh seed dormancy.

relationships.description: is the process by which the trait is passed
